quick_facts.label: Recruitment classification
quick_facts.value: Accounts Clerk cum Typist is an RRB Non-Technical Popular Categories (NTPC) undergraduate/10+2-level post—not a post requiring an undergraduate degree. The current-cycle CEN 07/2025 is titled for NTPC undergraduate posts.

quick_facts.label: Minimum education and typing
quick_facts.value: The RRB NTPC undergraduate notification framework requires 12th (+2 stage) or equivalent with 50% aggregate marks for this post, subject to stated exemptions; computer typing proficiency is essential. The qualifying typing benchmark is 30 wpm in English or 25 wpm in Hindi, without editing tools.

quick_facts.label: Selection route
quick_facts.value: For the typist posts, the RRB process uses CBT stages followed by a qualifying Computer-Based Typing Skill Test; candidates then proceed through document verification and the prescribed medical process if shortlisted. Exact stages, dates and conditions must be checked in the applicable CEN.

quick_facts.label: Pay level
quick_facts.value: In CEN 07/2025 context, Accounts Clerk cum Typist was notified at 7th CPC Pay Level 2 with initial basic pay of ₹19,900. This is basic pay, not a guaranteed take-home salary; allowances and deductions vary by posting and applicable rules.

quick_facts.label: Work setting
quick_facts.value: Accounts staff support the Railway Accounts Department, whose functions include keeping railway accounts, internal checking of receipts and expenditure, settling proper claims, budget work and management-accounting support. The department operates across headquarters, divisions, workshops, stores depots and construction projects.

sections.heading: What the role supports
paragraphs: An Accounts Clerk cum Typist is a non-gazetted clerical role within the Railway Accounts function. The job should be understood as supporting the department’s controlled financial-record and office workflow rather than independently exercising the statutory financial-adviser role held by Accounts officers. In practice, work can involve preparing and maintaining records, typing and processing official documentation, and assisting sections that handle bills, vouchers, receipts, expenditure or claims, depending on the unit and assigned seat.

The scope of the Accounts Department explains the context: it keeps railway accounts, performs internal checks on transactions affecting receipts and expenditure, settles proper claims, participates in budget control and provides financial/management information. Railway accounting rules also refer to voucher and bill handling, so accuracy, record discipline and adherence to procedures are central to the clerical support environment.
bullets: Maintain accurate accounts-office records and documentation assigned by the section.
Type official material on a computer and meet the prescribed typing-skill standard during recruitment.
Support transaction, bill, voucher, claim, receipt or expenditure-related workflows according to local allocation of duties and railway rules.
Follow internal-check, record-keeping and confidentiality requirements in a public-finance office.

sections.heading: Entry through RRB NTPC
paragraphs: The principal external route is an RRB NTPC undergraduate recruitment notification that includes Accounts Clerk cum Typist. The node description should therefore be read carefully: “undergraduate” in RRB NTPC nomenclature refers to the 10+2/12th-level stream, not necessarily to a bachelor’s-degree requirement. In the preceding official CEN 06/2024 and the subsequent CEN 07/2025 undergraduate cycle, this post was grouped with other NTPC undergraduate posts.

Applicants should read the detailed CEN for their cycle and chosen RRB rather than relying on old coaching material. The official CEN framework specifies the education requirement, age cut-off date, category relaxations, vacancy distribution, CBT scheme, typing test, document verification and medical standards. The 2025-cycle application window is historical context only and should not be treated as an open deadline.
bullets: Hold 12th (+2 stage) or an equivalent qualification from a recognised board; the notification framework specifies 50% aggregate, with exemptions stated in the CEN for SC/ST, PwBD, ex-servicemen and candidates holding a higher qualification.
Clear the computer-based examination stages prescribed in the applicable CEN.
For Accounts Clerk cum Typist, clear the qualifying computer typing test: 30 wpm in English or 25 wpm in Hindi.
Complete document verification and meet the medical standard specified for the notified post; CEN 07/2025 context lists C2 for this post.
Use only official RRB websites and the RRB application portal for live notices, corrections, exam-city information, call letters and results.

sections.heading: Skills to develop
paragraphs: The assessed skills are explicitly computer-based typing in English or Hindi, plus the knowledge and test performance required in the NTPC CBT stages. Beyond the examination, the financial-control environment makes careful data entry, numerical accuracy, orderly file and record management, and the ability to follow written procedures especially valuable.

Candidates should practise sustained, accurate typing on a personal-computer keyboard rather than treating speed alone as sufficient. Because the typing test is qualifying, a candidate’s progression in recruitment depends on satisfying the prescribed benchmark even though the test’s marks are not added to merit under the cited CEN framework.
bullets: English typing at 30 wpm or Hindi typing at 25 wpm on a personal computer.
Accuracy in figures, registers, bills, vouchers and supporting records.
Computer-based office documentation and disciplined data entry.
Reading and applying financial and administrative instructions.
Professional confidentiality and attention to internal-control procedures.

sections.heading: Where an Accounts Clerk cum Typist may work
paragraphs: The post is office-based, but the exact location is determined by the railway, zone, division and allocation after recruitment. The Indian Railways Accounts Code places Accounts officers and the supporting Accounts organisation at headquarters and in divisions, workshops, stores depots and construction projects. Consequently, an Accounts Clerk cum Typist may work in any of these administrative or operational-support locations rather than at a passenger-facing station role by default.

New staff receive role-relevant initial training in railway practice. A zonal railway training programme, for example, lists an initial course specifically for Junior Accounts Assistant/Accounts Clerk, while Northern Railway has published results for initial training examinations covering Accounts Clerk cum Typists and Junior Accounts Assistant cum Typists.
bullets: Zonal railway headquarters or divisional Accounts offices.
Workshop, stores-depot or construction-project Accounts units.
Sections dealing with payroll/cash and pay, bills, expenditure, traffic accounts, books, budget, pension/PF or related records, according to local organisation.
Initial training and departmental learning after appointment.

sections.heading: Career progression context
paragraphs: Progression depends on current Railway Board rules, eligibility, seniority, suitability/selection requirements, vacancies and any qualifying departmental conditions; it is not automatic merely because someone holds the entry post. A Railway grade-merger chart identifies Accounts Clerk as an entry-grade category and records promotion pathways in the Accounts clerical establishment, including Junior Accounts Assistant and higher Accounts Assistant/Senior Accounts Assistant categories. This is useful career context, but candidates and employees should verify the latest applicable establishment orders with their railway before making decisions.

The Accounts Department also offers continuing institutional learning: official zonal training material lists initial training for Accounts Clerks/Junior Accounts Assistants and refresher training for Accounts Assistants. This supports gradual movement from basic clerical processing toward more specialised accounting work where rules and selection permit.
bullets: Entry: Accounts Clerk cum Typist, Pay Level 2.
Potential internal advancement: Accounts clerical grades such as Junior Accounts Assistant and Accounts Assistant/Senior Accounts Assistant, subject to the prevailing promotion rules.
Development route: initial training, refresher training, service experience and any required departmental selection/qualification.
Do not assume a fixed time-to-promotion or a guaranteed grade sequence; these are establishment-rule and vacancy dependent.

sections.heading: Pay and benefits context
paragraphs: For the 2025 NTPC undergraduate notification cycle, Accounts Clerk cum Typist was listed at 7th CPC Pay Level 2 with ₹19,900 initial basic pay. This is the correct way to interpret commonly quoted salary figures: it is the notified starting basic pay, not the same as monthly net pay.

Actual gross and take-home remuneration can change with admissible central-government/railway allowances, posting location, pay revisions, tax and other deductions. Treat any online “in-hand salary” estimate as non-authoritative unless it is calculated from the employee’s applicable pay and deduction details. The official recruitment notice is the source to check for the pay level and initial basic pay in a particular cycle.
bullets: Dated recruitment context: CEN 07/2025 listed Level 2 and ₹19,900 initial basic pay.
Allowances and deductions are variable and should not be collapsed into a single guaranteed take-home figure.
Check the live detailed CEN and subsequent railway orders for future recruitment cycles or pay-rule changes.
